---
title: "Codex CLI"
description: "OpenAI Codex — builder agent on AWS, authenticated post-install."
---

<Note>
**Experimental pack.** Codex CLI is OpenAI's coding agent (not Bedrock). The pack installs the binary and configures it as a *builder agent* (`danger-full-access` sandbox, never-prompt approval). Authentication is deferred until after deploy — you run `codex login` or paste an API key on the instance.
</Note>

## When to use

- You want OpenAI's Codex as the agent
- You're OK with a post-deploy auth step (not fully headless)
- You want `codex exec "prompt"` one-shots or the full TUI

## What the pack installs

- Codex CLI via npm (user-owned prefix if possible; falls back to `sudo npm install -g`)
- `~/.codex/config.toml` with a managed block:
  ```toml
  model = "gpt-5.4"
  approval_policy = "never"
  sandbox_mode = "danger-full-access"
  ```
- No systemd service — pure CLI pack

The managed block is sentinel-isolated: any user edits *outside* the markers are preserved on re-install.

## Install — non-interactive

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Builder (recommended)">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli \
      --profile builder
    ```
    After install, SSM in and run `codex login`.
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Account assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli \
      --profile account_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="Personal assistant">
    ```bash
    curl -sfL install.lowkey.run | bash -s -- -y \
      --pack codex-cli \
      --profile personal_assistant
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

## Pack parameters

| Param | Flag | Default | Description |
|---|---|---|---|
| `region` | `--region` | `us-east-1` | AWS region (informational — Codex uses OpenAI's API) |
| `model` | `--model` | `gpt-5.4` | OpenAI model id (see [platform.openai.com/docs/models](https://platform.openai.com/docs/models)) |

<Warning>
Bedrock-style model IDs (`us.anthropic.*`, `eu.*`, `amazon.*`, etc.) are **rejected by the pack** with a warning — OpenAI's API returns HTTP 400 for them. This is a defense-in-depth guard; the normal CFN flow now passes `gpt-5.4` by default.
</Warning>

## Resource requirements

| | All profiles |
|---|---|
| Instance | `t4g.medium` |
| Root volume | 40 GB |
| Data volume | 0 GB |

## First-run (post-install)

Codex auth is intentionally deferred — the pack won't embed your OpenAI credentials in the AMI. Pick one:

<Tabs>
  <Tab title="Browser-based ChatGPT login (easiest)">
    ```bash
    aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
    codex login
    # → prints a URL and device code; enter in your browser
    ```
  </Tab>
  <Tab title="OpenAI API key">
    ```bash
    aws ssm start-session --target <instance-id>
    export OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-...
    # Or stdin:
    printenv OPENAI_API_KEY | codex login --with-api-key
    ```
  </Tab>
</Tabs>

Then verify:

```bash
codex --version
codex login status            # → "Logged in using ChatGPT"
codex exec "what is 2+3?"     # → 5
```

## Security: managed block sentinels

`~/.codex/config.toml` uses fenced markers so this pack plays nicely with your other Codex config:

```toml
# >>> managed by lowkey codex-cli pack >>>
model = "gpt-5.4"
approval_policy = "never"
sandbox_mode = "danger-full-access"
# <<< managed by lowkey codex-cli pack <<<

# anything BELOW here is yours — preserved on re-install
[projects."/home/ec2-user/work"]
trust_level = "trusted"
```

Re-running the pack regenerates the managed block (byte-identical if nothing changed) but leaves everything else alone.

## Tear-down

Delete the stack. Codex credentials on the instance vanish with the EBS volume — rotate your OpenAI key if the account is shared.
